name: Akeneo PIM API
what_it_does: Akeneo PIM REST API — Product Information Management system API for centralizing, enriching, and distributing product data across channels and catalogs, enabling agents to automate product data enrichment, category management, and multi-channel publishing.
use_cases: Agents enriching product data — automatically populating product descriptions, attributes, and metadata from external sources
Category management — agents organizing and restructuring product catalogs based on e-commerce taxonomy changes
Multi-channel publishing — agents triggering Akeneo product exports to Shopify, Magento, and marketplace channels
Data quality automation — agents identifying and flagging incomplete product records for human review and completion
Product import automation — agents bulk-importing new SKUs from supplier feeds into Akeneo with attribute mapping
not_for: Simple Shopify catalogs — Shopify's native product management is sufficient for small catalogs without PIM complexity
Order and inventory management — Akeneo manages product information only; use Shopify/ERP for inventory and orders
Real-time product updates — Akeneo is a batch-oriented system; use a headless commerce API for real-time catalog queries
best_when: You have complex product catalogs with thousands of SKUs, multiple channels, and rich product information that needs centralized management and syndication.
avoid_when: Your catalog has fewer than 1,000 SKUs — Akeneo's complexity is overkill; use Shopify or Contentful for simpler product management.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 client credentials with client ID and client secret. Access token via token endpoint. Tokens expire and require refresh. Connection (client) created per integration with configurable attribute/category ACLs.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Community Edition: free open source (self-hosted); Growth Edition: starts at ~$25K/year
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth: ~$25,000/year; Enterprise: ~$50,000+/year; Akeneo SaaS: custom pricing
pricing.notes: Community Edition (open source) is free for self-hosting. Commercial editions add cloud hosting, support, and enterprise features. SaaS pricing based on SKU volume and channels.

performance.rate_limits: 10 req/s per connection; bulk import via file upload; pagination required for large product sets

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive API documentation with OpenAPI spec. Error responses include property-level validation details. OAuth token management is standard. Pagination cursor required for large result sets. Self-hosted version docs apply equally to cloud.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product upsert by SKU is idempotent — same SKU creates or updates. Category creation by code is idempotent. All product attribute updates are idempotent — last value wins.
known_agent_gotchas: Family (product template) must exist before creating products — family defines required attributes for product completeness
Attribute codes are case-sensitive and immutable — incorrect code format causes silent data storage issues
Locale and channel-scoped attributes require locale and scope parameters — missing these returns empty values
Bulk operations use file upload (CSV/JSON) not REST — agents must switch to batch API pattern for large imports
Connection ACLs determine which attributes and categories are accessible — request correct ACLs during connection setup

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant with EU data residency. OAuth 2.0 with connection-level ACLs for attribute/category access control. Community Edition security depends on self-hosting practices. Enterprise grade for SaaS deployments.
